sourceMovie.title: Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween (2018) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ari Sandel's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Roald Dahl's The Witches
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween clicked for you, Roald Dahl's The Witches (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween?
faq.answer: Try Roald Dahl's The Witches, The House with a Clock in Its Walls, Shrek Forever After—each shares DNA with Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween?
faq.answer: Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween (2018) is best described as Comedy, Fantasy, Horror, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Goosebumps Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
